{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.3,

    "R1.4": 0.0,
    "R1.5": 0.7,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.5", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the improvement focus on (A) copy-editing only—sentence-level clarity, grammar, and concision without changing claim emphasis or ordering—or (B) a substantive abstract rewrite that also reframes the gap/contribution, tightens/hedges claims, and reorganizes the result sequence (no new data or citations added)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Improving the draft likely requires sharpening how the gap and contribution are framed at the abstract level.",
    "R1.5": "A clearer narrative arc may need sentence/block reordering to present motivation, methods, and findings coherently.",
    "R1.6": "The central request is to polish wording for grammar, concision, and flow.",
    "RX.1": "Edits should preserve or add appropriate caveats about generality and limits of the findings.",
    "RX.2": "The improved text must avoid overstating evidence or implying validation beyond what is reported.",
    "RX.3": "Work must stay within improving the provided text without introducing new results, claims, or citations.",
    "RX.4": "Wording should avoid recommendations that could encourage unsafe or misleading operational decisions.",
    "RX.5": "Any rephrasing must keep reported numbers and method descriptions traceable to the source text."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the improvement focus on (A) copy-editing only—sentence-level clarity, grammar, and concision without changing claim emphasis or ordering—or (B) a substantive abstract rewrite that also reframes the gap/contribution, tightens/hedges claims, and reorganizes the result sequence (no new data or citations added)?

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft likely requires sharpening how the gap and contribution are framed at the abstract level.
rationale.R1.5: A clearer narrative arc may need sentence/block reordering to present motivation, methods, and findings coherently.
rationale.R1.6: The central request is to polish wording for grammar, concision, and flow.
rationale.RX.1: Edits should preserve or add appropriate caveats about generality and limits of the findings.
rationale.RX.2: The improved text must avoid overstating evidence or implying validation beyond what is reported.
rationale.RX.3: Work must stay within improving the provided text without introducing new results, claims, or citations.
rationale.RX.4: Wording should avoid recommendations that could encourage unsafe or misleading operational decisions.
rationale.RX.5: Any rephrasing must keep reported numbers and method descriptions traceable to the source text.
